solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String reverseParentheses(String s) {
        Stack<StringBuilder> stack = new Stack<>();
        StringBuilder current = new StringBuilder();

        for (char c : s.toCharArray()) {
            if (c == '(') {
                stack.push(current);
                current = new StringBuilder();
            } else if (c == ')') {
                current = new StringBuilder(stack.pop().append(current.reverse()).toString());
            } else {
                current.append(c);
            }
        }

        return current.toString();
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n^2)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution uses a stack to efficiently reverse substrings within parentheses by maintaining a current string and reversing when encountering closing brackets. The stack allows for nested reversal handling. The main weakness is the O(n^2) time complexity due to string reversal operations.
